title: arctostaphylos montana subsp. montana
intro: Arctostaphylos montana subsp. montana, also known as mountain bearberry, is a perennial shrub native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The health benefits of mountain bearberry include its ability to support urinary tract health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Mountain bearberry is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and can be used to treat issues such as urinary tract infections, wounds, and skin irritations.
